title: Olin Garn
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Olin.jpg]] |name=Olin Garn |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' manual |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him{{LucasArts|url=products/alliance/xwa_characters.htm|text=XWA: Characters}} |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Dark |cyber= |families=[[Azzameen family]] |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' **[[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Hey, man, I was really sorry to hear about what happened to your family. But I'm glad you've decided to join the Rebellion! Just remember, this isn't about revenge. It's about fighting injustice and tyranny! We're going to bring the Empire down! And put an end to tragedies like what happened to your family!|Olin Garn, to Ace Azzameen|audio=OlinGarn.flac|}} '''Olin Garn''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who frequently contracted with [[Twin Suns Transport Services/Legends|Twin Suns Transport Services]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. While working for the [[Azzameen family]], Garn developed a close friendship with [[Aeron Azzameen]] and soon became accepted as one of the family. By the time of the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Garn decided to join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], serving as a pilot in the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]]. He remained in contact with the Azzameens, serving as their contact when Aeron's father, [[Tomaas Azzameen/Legends|Tomaas]], arranged to deliver a supply of [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] to the Rebellion. Soon after, Tomaas and his eldest son, [[Galin Azzameen/Legends|Galin]], were [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and the surviving family members declared traitors. When Tomaas's children, Aeron, [[Emon Azzameen|Emon]], and [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace]], sought sanctuary with the Alliance, Garn vouched for the group and recommended Ace Azzameen for training as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] pilot. In the months leading up to the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Garn was stationed aboard the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']]. During this time, he fought alongside Ace Azzameen in several missions, including the capture of the Imperial [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Kupalo]], the rescue of several [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] operatives whose [[Starship/Legends|ship]] had been detained by the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Emancipator|''Accuser'']], and [[Battle of Sullust (4 ABY)|escorting]] the Star Cruisers [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] and [[Defiance/Legends|''Defiance'']] as they rendezvoused with the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] near [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]]. ==Biography== ===Joining the Rebellion=== {{Quote|I finally got a message from Olin. I'm all for the Rebellion, but it plays havoc with relationships. Not that Olin and I have one. Oh, forget it.|Aeron Azzameen, in an e-mail to Ace Azzameen|}} During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Olin Garn worked as a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and freelance [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]. By the early [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], he was working on a contractual basis for [[Tomaas Azzameen/Legends|Tomaas]] and [[Antan Azzameen]], owners of the shipping company [[Twin Suns Transport Services/Legends|Twin Suns Transport Services]]. While working for the [[Azzameen family]], he developed a close friendship with Tomaas's daughter, [[Aeron Azzameen|Aeron]], and the entire family came to consider him as one of their own. Around the time of the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Garn decided to leave his work with the Azzameens and join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. His experience in flying freighters led him to become a pilot in the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]], and he was assigned to the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Defiance/Legends|''Defiance'']] with the rank of [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]]. His infrequent leave allowed him little time to spend with Aeron, but Garn stayed in contact with her through [[e-mail]] and gave her the coordinates of the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] in case she needed to contact him in an emergency. Shortly after leaving, he contacted Tomaas's youngest son, [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace]], asking him to keep an eye on Aeron for him and encouraging the young pilot to practice his skills and consider joining the Rebellion. When Garn learned of an increase in [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] activity in the region, he contacted the Azzameens again, warning them to stay alert for any trouble. ===Reunion with the Azzameens=== {{Quote|Congratulations on your first Imperial kill! I heard about your first tangle with the Imperials. Well, you're gonna tangle a lot more now that you've joined the Rebellion. If that first kill is bothering you, just remember what we're fighting for.|Olin Garn, in an e-mail to Ace Azzameen|}} [[File:GarnXWA1.png|thumb|left|220px|Olin Garn's T-65 X-wing starfighter]] Following the Battle of Hoth, during which Imperial forces under the command of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] forced the Rebels to evacuate their [[Echo Base/Legends|base]] on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], the Alliance sent many of those wounded in the battle to their [[XQ2 Platform]] [[Hospital (XQ2 Platform)|''Hospital'']] for treatment. Having had problems dealing with [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] groups in the past, the Rebels approached Tomaas Azzameen to act as a middleman in acquiring a supply of [[Black market/Legends|black market]] [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] with which to treat the wounded. After the Azzameens picked up the bacta from their supplier, they arranged to deliver it to the Alliance. Due to his experience with the family, Garn was chosen to act as their contact and a rendezvous was arranged at [[Brint-wo Colony]]. Flying a lone [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]], Garn met with the Azzameens and led them to ''Hospital''. The pilot had little time to catch up with his friends, however, and returned to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] as Tomaas's [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]] ''[[Vasudra]]'' began to transfer its cargo to ''Hospital''. Soon after Garn's departure, the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Corrupter (Imperial-class)|''Corrupter'']] [[Battle of Hospital|interrupted]] the cargo transfer. The Rebels were forced to evacuate, along with Ace and [[Emon Azzameen]], who had been escorting their father, but the ''Vasudra'' was unable to escape, and both Tomaas and [[Galin Azzameen/Legends|Galin Azzameen]] were killed. The Empire soon [[Imperialization of Azzameen assets|declared]] the entire Azzameen family traitors and issued a warrant for their arrest. The surviving members of the family attempted to escape to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], but Antan was captured in an attack by their rivals, the [[Viraxo]]. With their options limited, Tomaas's three surviving children, Ace, Aeron, and Emon, decided to seek refuge with the Rebel Alliance. Using the coordinates that Garn had supplied to Aeron, the Azzameens were able to locate the ''Defiance'' and were given permission to dock. Though there was some suspicion of the group following recent events, Garn vouched for them, and his superiors agreed to offer them sanctuary. Though the elder Azzameen children soon left in search of their uncle, Garn was impressed that Ace had achieved his first Imperial kill during the skirmish at ''Hospital'' and decided to recommend him for training as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] pilot. Following Garn's recommendation, the ''Defiance''{{'s}} tactical officer, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Devers (Rebel Alliance)|Devers]], arranged for Azzameen to take part in simulated [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat]] trials. Garn was assigned as Azzameen's instructor during both of his trials. The first assessed his performance in deep [[Realspace/Legends|space]] strike missions, using a [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighter]] to conduct a series of [[Bomb/Legends|bombing]] raids against Imperial targets. The [[Starfighter Superiority Evaluation|second trial]] tested Azzameen's ability to dogfight against [[TIE series/Legends|Imperial starfighters]], both individually and teamed with other Alliance pilots from [[Green Squadron (Rebel Alliance)|Green Squadron]], including Garn. As part of this, the Rebel pilots engaged TIE fighters launched from the [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Marut]]''. Garn later praised Azzameen's relentless attacks on the Star Destroyer itself.[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] Impressed by Azzameen's performance in both trials, Garn cleared him for active duty. ===Transfer to the ''Liberty''=== {{Quote|No scumbag traitor can get away from us, eh? LOL. I guess we showed that Kupalo character how to fly. Poor fool, he's probably still getting his brain zapped right now. It was good flying with you, kid.|Olin Garn, in an e-mail to Ace Azzameen|}} Shortly thereafter, the Alliance transferred several pilots from the ''Defiance'' to the Mon Calamari Star Cruiser [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']] to replace those lost in a recent operation. Garn and Azzameen were among those sent to serve on the ''Liberty''. Unbeknown to the Rebels, the ''Liberty''{{'s}} tactical officer, Commander [[Kupalo]], who had been captured by the Empire at Hoth, had been brainwashed to serve as an Imperial agent and had been feeding information to the Empire since his recent rescue by Alliance forces. After the ''Liberty'' oversaw the [[Battle of Eidoloni|defection]] of Commander [[Zaletta]] to the Alliance, Kupalo revealed his true nature by stealing Zaletta's [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]] ''[[AA-23]]'' and attempting to return it to the Empire before the Rebels could examine the information contained in its [[Computer/Legends|computer]]. Kupalo was able to destroy three X-wings which were sent to intercept him and escaped into hyperspace. Garn and Azzameen were quickly scrambled and sent after him in [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptors]] with orders to prevent him from reaching Imperial forces and to ensure his recapture. [[File:GarnXWA2.png|thumb|right|220px|Garn engages an ''Alpha''-class Xg-1 Star Wing.]] The Rebel pilots pursued Kupalo through a number of jump points, but each time they approached his [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], Kupalo released homing [[Mine/Legends|mines]] to delay them while he jumped to hyperspace. When they finally caught up with him, the Rebels emerged from hyperspace some distance from Kupalo's shuttle moments before Imperial [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|''Alpha''-class Xg-1 Star Wings]] arrived in the area. Garn, leading the mission, moved to intercept the Assault Gunboats and ordered Azzameen to continue the pursuit of Kupalo. When Kupalo ignored Garn's order to stand down, Azzameen fired on the fleeing shuttle, causing damage to its hull and forcing the commander to surrender. The Alliance's [[Storm Unit (team)|Storm Unit]] arrived aboard their [[Storm Unit (Gamma-class ATR-6 assault transport)|assault transport]] to board and capture the shuttle, but the operation was interrupted by the arrival of the Star Destroyer [[Imperator (Imperial-class)|''Imperator'']], which began launching TIEs. However, Garn and Azzameen were able to hold off the Imperial starfighters long enough for Storm Unit to capture the shuttle. After Storm Unit departed, the Rebel pilots followed them back to the ''Liberty''. Garn and Azzameen were highly praised by their superiors, including Admiral Yamarus, for their part in Kupalo's capture. Following Kupalo's recapture, Garn informed Aeron that [[Ororo Transportation/Legends|Ororo Transportation's]] [[Vergesso Base|base]] in the [[Vergesso Asteroids/Legends|Vergesso Asteroids]] was critically short of supplies and was willing to pay a high price to anybody who could deliver them. Based on this information, Aeron and Ace launched an operation to steal supplies from the Viraxo [[industrial complex]] [[VXO-33274]]. Aeron decided to remain at Vergesso Base for a while, but Garn soon learned that it had come under [[Battle of the Bajic Shipyards|Imperial attack]] and, unaware that Aeron was aboard the station, warned Ace to stay away from the [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]]. Ace was able to reach the base in time and rescue his sister. Zaletta, who had taken Kupalo's place as the ''Liberty''{{'s}} tactical officer, provided intelligence indicating that several large convoys had been traveling to a large Imperial construction project and that one such convoy would soon be passing through the [[Eidoloni system/Legends|Eidoloni system]]. In order to ascertain the nature of the project, [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] ordered a reconnaissance flight. Flying as [[Blue Leader/Legends|Blue Leader]], with Azzameen as his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmate]] in a pair of A-wings, Garn was dispatched to the Eidoloni [[Star system/Legends|system]] with orders to determine the composition and cargo of the convoy. The Rebels arrived shortly before the convoy—several [[Xizor Transport Systems/Legends|Xizor Transport Systems]] freighters escorted by the [[Victory II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory II''-class Star Destroyers]] [[Protector (Victory II-class)|''Protector'']] and [[Vanguard (Victory II-class)|''Vanguard'']], along with the [[Lancer-class frigate/Legends|''Lancer''-class frigates]] [[Furious (Lancer-class)|''Furious'']] and ''[[Black Hawk]]''. When [[TIE/ad starfighter|TIE Avengers]] moved to intercept the Rebel fighters, Garn engaged them, successfully keeping most of the Imperial fighters occupied while Azzameen scanned the convoy. Their assignment complete, the two Rebels chose to disengage and returned to the ''Liberty''. ===Countdown to Endor=== {{Quote|Well, if it isn't pilots Garn and Azzameen! Glad you could join us. I trust you're both doing well?''"
"''Good to see you, Commander. Don't worry, we've got you covered.|Devers and Olin Garn|audio=DeversGarn-XWA.flac|}} The convoy's cargo of [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] and other raw materials led Alliance High Command to suspect that the Empire was constructing a new base of operations or [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]], and uncovering the nature of the project was made a top priority. Information provided by the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] crime syndicate eventually led the Rebels to the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]], where the Empire was constructing a [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Spy/Legends|spies]] were sent to the Endor system to gather information about the construction site, but as they were returning to the Alliance [[Fleet/Legends|Fleet]], their ships were stopped, along with several civilian vessels, by the ''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer [[Emancipator|''Accuser'']] and the [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruiser]] [[Grappler (Immobilizer 418 cruiser)|''Grappler'']]. [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Yamarus]], commander of the ''Liberty'', dispatched three X-wings from [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadron]] and three A-wings from [[Red Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Red Squadron]] to attack the ''Grappler'' in order to allow the Bothans to escape. [[File:GarnXWA3.png|thumb|left|240px|Garn fires a proton torpedo at the Immobilizer 418 cruiser ''Grappler''.]] Arriving in the area as the ''Accuser'' began searching the waiting vessels, Garn, commanding the X-wings, feared that the Rebels had arrived too late to rescue the Bothan ships. Nevertheless, he ordered his group to target the ''Grappler'' and launch their [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]]. Though the Rebels came under attack from Imperial fighters, Garn's group continued their attack until the ''Grappler'' was forced to withdraw. Seeing their chance to flee, the Bothan and civilian ships attempted to escape to hyperspace, prompting the ''Accuser'' to open fire on them and kill many of the Bothans. The Rebel fighters fought [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] launched by the ''Accuser'' until the surviving Bothans left the area. With their mission complete, the Rebels returned to the ''Liberty''. After learning that the Death Star was not yet completed and that Emperor Palpatine was due to oversee the final stages of construction, the Alliance Fleet began to gather near [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] in preparation for a [[Battle of Endor/Legends|major offensive]] against the battlestation.{{Film|VI}} Garn's duties temporarily led him away from the ''Liberty'', but he made sure to return before the anticipated battle. As the Empire searched for the Rebel forces, the ''Liberty'' received word that the task forces led by the Mon Calamari Star Cruisers ''Defiance'' and [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] had both been involved in skirmishes with Imperial patrols. With the arrival of both task forces vital to the Alliance's plans, Garn was [[Battle of Sullust (4 ABY)|sent]] to lead a group of four A-wings from Gold Squadron in seeing them safely to their last hyperspace jump on the way to Sullust. The group first rendezvoused with the [[Defiance Task Force]] shortly before the arrival of the ''Victory''-class Star Destroyer ''[[Vagrant]]'' and the [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|''Carrack''-class light cruisers]] [[Nexus (Carrack-class)|''Nexus'']] and [[Xerxes (Carrack-class)|''Xerxes'']]. The X-wings intercepted TIE bombers and Assault Gunboats, preventing them from launching torpedoes at the ''Defiance'' until it and its task force jumped to hyperspace. With no time to waste, Garn led the group back to the ''Liberty'' and made another jump to join Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar's]] [[Independence task force|task force]]. When they arrived, the Star Destroyers ''[[Immortal/Legends|Immortal]]'', ''Protector'', and ''Vanguard'' were already in the area and launching TIE bombers against the ''Independence''. Garn's group fought off several waves of bombers, only returning to the ''Liberty'' once the ''Independence'' and its escorts escaped. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Hey, Ace. There's no doubt in anyone's mind that major debris is about to fill space. The Empire's on the move…the Alliance is on the move. Man, I think we're in for a lot of combat duty. Try and keep Aeron out of it. I don't want to see her hurt.|Olin Garn, in an e-mail to Ace Azzameen|}} [[File:Aeron.jpg|thumb|Garn became close with Aeron Azzameen (pictured).]] Garn was a firm believer in the Rebel cause and felt that ending Imperial tyranny for all was more important than any personal desire for revenge. Though his work required him to take the lives of others, Garn was able to overcome any remorse for his actions by reminding himself what he was fighting for and recommended that Ace Azzameen do the same after he joined the Alliance. Garn was always eager to be involved in combat missions and, when his duties led him away from the ''Liberty'' shortly before the Battle of Endor, he made sure to return before the Rebels launched their assault on the Death Star. In a combat situation, Garn could remain composed even when faced with vastly superior firepower. During his time contracting for the Azzameen family, Garn became close with both Aeron and Ace. After leaving to join the Rebels, he stayed in touch with both, encouraging Ace to practice his piloting in the hopes that the youngest Azzameen would one day join him in fighting the Empire and asking Ace to watch over Aeron in his absence. Garn's relationship with Aeron was made difficult by his decision to join the Rebellion; his duties left him little leave time to visit her. When the Azzameens fled to the Alliance after the death of their father and brother, Garn convinced his superiors that they could be trusted and was instrumental in helping Ace to be accepted as a starfighter pilot. The Azzameen family's [[MK-series maintenance droid]] [[MK-09/Legends|MK-09]] considered Garn reliable and less stubborn than Ace and believed that Garn would help to keep him out of trouble. ==Skills and abilities== Garn was a competent pilot, the abilities he developed flying freighters serving him well after joining the Rebels, where he was frequently selected to lead combat missions. He regularly tested his abilities against his peers in the [[pilot proving grounds]] and posted times that compared well to some of the Alliance's best pilots. ==Behind the scenes== Olin Garn was created for the [[1999]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'', in which he was voiced by [[Steve Blum|Steven Jay Blum]]. The character served as a friend and [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmate]] of the player, in the role of Ace Azzameen. Due to Garn's role in the game's story, his starfighter is set as indestructible in the missions in which he is featured. The character's backstory was expanded in a short biography in the game's manual and on the LucasArts website. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Olin Garn}} *{{LucasArts|url=products/alliance/xwa_characters.htm|text=XWA: Characters}} *[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{InsiderCite|152|The Rebel Bruiser: Steve Blum is Zeb Orrelios}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Olin Garn |es=Olin Garn |pl=Olin Garn }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Garn, Olin}} [[Category:Blue Squadron personnel]] [[Category:Bounty hunters]] [[Category:Green Squadron personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pilots]] [[Category:Twin Suns Transport Services personnel]]
